event_info: on a length outside off; shapes back in slightly as Travis Head steers it towards point
on a length around leg; Travis Head swings early and the inside edge flicks his pads and rolls towards short fine leg. First run of the match

not out! RCB lose a review in the first over of the match. A back of a length delivery outside off; shapes back away as Abhishek Sharma gets beaten on the outside edge. UltraEdge confirms the same as the bowler starts walking back to his run-up mark

first maximum of IPL 2026! Full and wide outside off; Abhishek Sharma reaches out for it with a slice; gets enough elevation as the ball flies over the fielder at deep backward point. He's off the mark in style
good length delivery outside off; Abhishek Sharma has a swing and a miss. Got beaten due to the movement
that's a juicy full toss outside off; Abhishek Sharma plays a well-timed cover drive; but straight to the fielder stationed at cover-point
good length delivery around middle and leg; Travis Head clips it through the leg-side for a single
advances down the wicket and knocks the length delivery in front of cover-point for a quick run
short of a length outside off; shaping away from the batter as Travis Head runs it down to third man for one
fired down the leg-side by Bhuvneshwar Kumar on the shorter side; called a wide by the umpire
short and quick around leg; Abhishek Sharma gets cramped for room as he looks to swipe this away; gets hit on the thigh pad as the ball lobs towards the keeper
on the shorter side and down the leg-side; another loose delivery by Bhuvneshwar Kumar. Jitesh Sharma had to move across by a long way to gather this; it was so wayward
1 run; cramped up for room again; off the thigh pad towards the leg-side as they take a leg bye

chopped on; almost! Just the kind of luck you need to get going in this tournament. Back of a length delivery around off; was too close to the body; Travis Head looks to slice it away and gets an inside edge that misses the stumps and races away to the fine leg boundary
yet another wide. They have bowled too many wides already. Another wayward ball down the leg-side

ball_event: out Caught by Jitesh Sharma!! Abhishek's poor run continues. He hasn't been at his best since a couple of months. This was a short ball by Jacob Duffy and Abhishek Sharma got hurried up due to the pace. He goes for a pull
event_info: losing his shape in the process. A thick top edge is induced and Jitesh calls for it and circles under the skier to complete a safe catch. A disappointing start for the destructive opener in IPL 2026. Abhishek Sharma c Jitesh Sharma b Jacob Duffy 7(8) [6s-1];
short and wide outside off; steered away through backward point
on a length outside off; punched towards cover by Travis Head
short of a length around off; shapes back in and skids along the deck; Travis Head looks to swipe it across the line; gets beaten on the inside edge

stand and deliver! On a length outside off; tad slower; Travis Head stays put and thumps it over mid-off. Didn't middle it to perfection; otherwise this would have sailed over the boundary. Good strike; nevertheless

ball_event: out Caught by Phil Salt!! Two wickets in the over for Jacob Duffy. What a superb debut for him for RCB already. A short ball by the pacer
event_info: Travis Head goes for a flat pull and Phil Salt at deep backward square leg takes a well-judged reverse cup catch. Both the destructive openers are back to the pavilion within the powerplay. The crowd at Chinnaswamy is absolutely loving this. Travis Head c Phil Salt b Jacob Duffy 11(9) [4s-2];
sublime timing! Back of a length outside off; Ishan Kishan stands tall and punches this through cover-point. Finds the gap as the ball absolutely races away to the fence
tad short outside off; Ishan Kishan rides the bounce and pats it in front of cover-point. Takes a quick run
lovely delivery by Bhuvneshwar Kumar outside off; shaping away from the batter who feels for it and almost nicks it behind
good length delivery around off; skids along the deck as Nitish Kumar Reddy taps it into the off-side
short and wide outside off; Nitish Kumar Reddy cuts it towards point where Duffy puts in a dive to his left and grabs it
back of a length around middle and leg; clips it towards mid-wicket for a single to end the over
tad short around off; some sharp carry as Nitish Kumar Reddy pats it towards point

ball_event: out Caught by Abhinandan Singh!! This is a dream debut for Jacob Duffy. He goes short
event_info: he goes quick and the batter gets hurried. Tries to go across the line; swings with lack of footwork and the top edge has been taken at mid-wicket. Abhinandan takes it stylishly and throws the ball away in swagger. Sunrisers Hyderabad in a spot of bother at the Chinnaswamy. They are three down within the powerplay.. Nitish Reddy c Abhinandan Singh b Jacob Duffy 1(6);
fraction short outside off; shaping away from Heinrich Klaasen who lets it through to the keeper
on a length outside off; Heinrich Klaasen drives this through covers; finds the gap and opens his account. Three runs to get going
fraction short outside off; some extra carry as Ishan Kishan steers this through backward point for one
end of another fantastic over by Jacob Duffy. Drifting on the pads as Heinrich Klaasen clips it with soft hands towards the on-side
short; quick and at the body. Ishan Kishan loses his shape while going for the pull shot; miscues it towards square leg; but it just falls in front of the fielder stationed there

that's the kind of form he has been in. More like it. A short ball on leg; Ishan Kishan picks the length early and pulls it over deep backward square leg for a 89m maximum. SRH need their captain to play with a similar intent tonight
more runs! Banged in short by Abhinandan Singh; Ishan Kishan goes for a pull over mid-wicket and manages to beat Duffy's chase. He put in a dive in the deep; but couldn't quite get there

Abhinandan Singh proving to be very expensive in his first over. This was a short and quick delivery outside off; Ishan Kishan slashes hard at it and the top edge flies right over third man. Hits the ad hoardings beyond the boundary
that was a juicy full toss; Ishan Kishan slices it away; but can't beat the fielder stationed inside the circle on the off-side
16 runs from that over! Back of a length around middle and leg; nudged towards mid-wicket by Ishan Kishan
a short ball by Jacob Duffy and Heinrich Klaasen has pulled it towards deep mid-wicket on the bounce
Jacob Duffy goes short outside off; Ishan Kishan rides the bounce; steering it towards third man for one
fraction short outside off; Heinrich Klaasen steers it through cover-point for one
short of a length around off; Kishan looks to defend; gets an inside edge onto the thigh pad; the ball rolls towards short third

almost another wicket! Heinrich Klaasen closes the bat face early and looks to nudge this away; ends up getting a leading edge for being early in his shot. The leading edge flies straight back to the bowler; and lands just in front of him

end of a superb spell on his RCB debut! Goes short on middle and leg; as Heinrich Klaasen pulls it towards deep square leg on the bounce. The RCB crowd give him a huge cheer at the end of his spell; bowled four overs on the trot. Has done his job with the ball for his new IPL franchise - he has been replaced by Padikkal who is the impact sub
on a length around middle and leg; driven down the ground to long-on
shot! Back of a length outside off; Ishan Kishan gets on top of it and stylishly carves it away through backward point. No chance of stopping that as Kishan adds four more to the tally
good length delivery outside off as Ishan Kishan plays this through covers for a single

yet another boundary. Romario Shepherd goes full around off; Heinrich Klaasen goes deep inside the crease and plays a well-timed off-drive. Beats the fielder inside the circle comfortably and finds the fence with ease
replica of the previous shot! Romario Shepherd goes full again and Heinrich Klaasen executes his off-drive once again. Same shot; same execution; and same result

just the kind of partnership SRH needed to steady the ship. These two batters are exactly doing that. This was fraction short and Heinrich Klaasen swiped it towards wide long-on. Salt in the deep does extremely well to stop the ball which was travelling and saves a boundary
Abhinandan Singh goes short outside off as Ishan Kishan drags it towards the on-side for one
full toss on the pads; Heinrich Klaasen clips it towards wide long-on for one
short in length around off; Ishan Kishan looks to drag this across the line; gets an inside edge onto the pads

smashed! Abhinandan Singh goes short outside off; this was on the slower side; Ishan Kishan picks the length and pace pretty well; rocks back in the crease and pulls it over mid-wicket. There were a couple of fielders converging in the deep; but he just managed to bisect them
worked through the leg-side for a couple of runs
50-run stand up! Pitched up by the bowler outside off; Ishan Kishan looks to squeeze it out; gets an inside edge past the stumps for a single
starts with a googly outside off; Ishan Kishan plays it late; steering it towards backward point

tossed up delivery outside off; slightly widish; Ishan Kishan reaches out for it with a drive and manages to beat Tim David's dive in the deep. The timing and placement were good enough to fetch him four runs
flatter through the air outside off; played through the off-side for a single

fraction short outside off; skids along the deck as Heinrich Klaasen punches it towards extra cover. The fielder stationed there puts in a dive to his right; gets his hand to it; eventually parrying it towards long-off
drags this slightly short ball through backward square leg as the fielder (Kanishk Chouhan) in the deep does well to stop the ball; keeps them down to two
driven back to the bowler to end the over
fired in flat on off; zips through from back of a length; Klaasen is cramped on the cut and mistimes it to short cover. He throws his head back in frustration. Not happy with himself
length ball sliding in on middle; Klaasen punches to long-off

stays round the wicket for the left-hander and angles it away outside off. Oh he bowled it with a very round-arm release while also bending his knees to keep the trajectory low. It's fired wide outside off; Kishan flat-bats to long-off
pushed down to long-off
angled away wide of off; good length ball; Kishan cuts it to deep point
driven firmly down the ground; it's in the air and Krunal dives across for the catch but it's out of his reach. Was much slower through the air at 88kph this delivery
fielder is very early into the dive but still manages to cut it off. Full on the pads; drifting in nicely and Klaasen clips it gently to the left of short fine
drilled down the ground! It's a googly from Suyash but it's too full and Kishan isn't going to let that go unpunished. Brings up his fifty in style!
again the same error. Length is too full and Kishan pounces. Only difference to the previous delivery was that the line was outside off and he went over extra cover instead of going down the ground

mix-up in the running but Klaasen survives! He responded to the initial call of a single from Kishan who later pulled out. Could've been trouble with a better throw but no damage done in the end. Kishan came dancing down the pitch and found the fielder at cover with his drive. Was no single there
can't find the gap as he cuts to backward point
terrible delivery and it disappears to the boundary in no time. Third one of the over. Dragged down short; perhaps anticipated a charge from Kishan but he hangs back and pulls hard behind square
oh that's a gift! Proper gift and nicely wrapped too with a bow on top. Juicy full toss; Klaasen's eyes light up and he smokes it over midwicket
fired in on a good length just outside off; Klaasen cuts through point
flat around off; back of a length; Kishan goes hard on the pull and mistimes it through midwicket
back of a length on middle and off; Klaasen makes some room and cuts to sweeper cover

the round-arm delivery from Krunal and this time it's dispatched over the ropes! Along with being round-arm; was also the 25-yarder from Krunal. Bowled it from well behind the crease and kept it on the pads. But Kishan quickly collapsed his back knee to get under the ball for a big slog-sweep. He's on fire out there!
now goes down the ground and fetches another six! Krunal is wondering where to bowl to him. Goes full outside off; it's in the slot and it gets hammered straight back

ball_event: out Caught by Phil Salt!! Salt is celebrating but he was very close to the ropes there. Umpire will send it upstairs to check if the catch is clean. Back of a length
event_info: heaved across the line and goes very high. It's Salt at the midwicket boundary and he goes down while taking the catch; doesn't stay on his feet. Replays don't seem very conclusive - very hard to say if some part of his back is touching the cushions. I don't see any movement of the cushions; says the third umpire. OUT flashes on the big screen; much to the delight of the partisan home crowd. Klaasen doesn't seem too pleased. He's having a word with the fourth umpire before making his way to the dugout. Heinrich Klaasen c Phil Salt b Romario Shepherd 31(22) [4s-2 6s-1];
tucked gently into the on-side
dug in short; Arora pulls it in the air and it falls just short of deep square leg who was charging in from the boundary
short of length outside off; angling away from over the wicket; Kishan cuts it to sweeper cover

the top-edge flies all the way and Shepherd has a big smile on his face. Can't believe his luck. Dug in short; Arora takes it on again and once again he's not in control of the pull. Gets big on him and flies over fine leg
back of a length in the channel; steered through point
right in the slot but Arora misses out. Drags it off a thick inside edge behind square. Was floated up from Suyash who missed his length again; like he did very often in his previous over
googly across the left-hander; good length ball; Kishan taps it through the off-side

ball_event: out Caught by Devdutt Padikkal !! Not very convincing from Arora in his short stay. Gets dismissed off a half-tracker. Was there to be pulled into the stands but he goes very half-heartedly and finds the fielder at deep midwicket. Good catch from Padikkal moving forward. Salil Arora c Devdutt Padikkal b Suyash Sharma 9(6) [6s-1]

event_info: flat and short outside off; Aniket looks to cut and is beaten
nicely played! Googly outside off; there's width on offer but Aniket doesn't go too hard. Simply opens the face and steers it past short third. Right in the gap between backward point and short third
almost a bouncer from Suyash. Very short outside off; speed is high as well; Aniket looks to pull and hits it down to long-on
good length on off; defended into the off-side

oh he got into such a great position! Abhinandan bashes it on a short of length and Aniket pounces! His weight is on the back foot as he pulls it for a flat six over midwicket. Was barely a short-arm jab but the timing was awesome
cracking yorker around off; squeezed to backward point
this time he gets rushed by the short ball. It zips through; hurries onto him and he gets it high on the bat while going for the pull. It's miscued in the air but lands safely wide of deep midwicket
such special batting! Just another level from Kishan! Angling away outside off; back of a length; he opens the face and carves it over short third. Flies over the fielder and then over the ropes

ball_event: out Caught by Phil Salt!! Phil Salt
event_info: you beauty! It needed something special to get rid of Kishan tonight and he has produced just that. A one-handed screamer running across at the point boundary when the ball seemed destined for the ropes. Full toss outside off; Kishan reaches out; opens the face and slices it in the air. It's well wide of Salt who charges to his right before reaching out one-handed at full stretch. Incredible work. Ishan Kishan c Phil Salt b Abhinandan Singh 80(38) [4s-8 6s-5];

makes this look so effortless! Back of a length; pace off perhaps and it sits up around the waist on middle and leg. From there it's all about the elegance of Aniket. Swivels across beautifully and pulls it over fine leg without going too hard. Pure timing!
tucked away into the on-side
close to the blockhole just outside off; sliced through point
dug in short just outside off; pace off; Aniket pulls it to deep square leg
slower bumper angled away outside off; left alone by Dubey and the umpire reckons it's outside the marker
short of length outside off; pace off again and Dubey drags the pull shot through midwicket. It's in the gap and he's back for two

ball_event: out Caught by Devdutt Padikkal!! Looks like Shepherd has outsmarted Dubey here. He shuffled across the stumps premeditatedly
event_info: probably expecting the line to be wide. But what he got was a very full ball on leg stump. Flicks it in the air and finds the fielder at fine leg. Harsh Dubey c Devdutt Padikkal b Romario Shepherd 3(3);
pace off looks like the way to go in these last few overs for RCB. Slower one outside off; back of a length and Aniket cuts it to sweeper cover

the slower bouncer from Bhuvi and it's been ramped over the keeper. Aniket initially took a couple of steps down the pitch; which may have prompted Bhuvi to go short. But he still managed to adjust so well

dropped by Kohli! Ran in from long-on; got to it but the ball burst right through his hands and landed on the thigh. He looks surprised more than anything. Was full in the slot; Aniket tried a no-look shot but miscued it high up. Lucky to get away!
short of length outside off ; pace off; Harshal punches it to cover

ball_event: out Caught by Devdutt Padikkal!! Straight down the throat of Padikkal at long-off. No mistake from him and Harshal has to go. Short of length outside off
event_info: he tries lofting off the back foot; not an easy shot and there's not enough on it to clear the ropes. The wickets keep tumbling. Harshal Patel c Devdutt Padikkal b Bhuvneshwar Kumar 0(2);
pace on this time; good length at the stumps; Payne knocks it down to long-off
finds the blockhole; Payne can't do much except squeeze it down to long-on
massive hit down the ground! In the slot around off; he gets under it and whacks it over long-on. Nice stable base to go with a hefty swing
tries the slower short ball this time but it's all the same for Aniket who spots it early; waits for it and plays the upper-cut to perfection. Almost goes all the way over backward point
not great bowling. Again in the slot; misses the yorker by a long way and Aniket takes full toll. Giving SRH just the finish they want. Smashes it down the ground for six more

review taken by RCB for a wide call! It's full and wide; Aniket tries a reverse-scoop but misses out. He had walked across the stumps and was standing well outside off when the ball was released. The revised wide marker has moved accordingly; and it's well inside the line according to ball-tracking. Good review!

ball_event: out Caught by Virat Kohli!! Was he caught in two minds? Looked like a very half-hearted chip down the ground. Was probably thinking if he should take a single to keep strike or go for the big hit. Ended up chipping it to long-off. Was full in the slot
event_info: there to be punished; but it results in his dismissal. Simple catch for Kohli at long-off. Aniket has a smile on his face as he walks off. Aniket Verma c Virat Kohli b Romario Shepherd 43(18) [4s-3 6s-4];
keeps it wide outside off; Payne goes after it and gets it away in the gap through point
again wide outside off; Payne slices it wide of sweeper cover; back for two more
1 run; now the yorker angled back in; catches Payne by surprise; his head falls over and he's hit on the pad. Was missing leg!
full and straight; driven firmly down the ground and long-off does well to cut it off
133kph; nails the yorker at the stumps; Unadkat squeezes it back down the pitch
close to the blockhole on off; Unadkat can't get under it but he drives wide of long-off and is back for two. Very poor throw from the fielder there. Should've been a run-out but the throw is so wide
short of a length around off; Philip Salt defends it into the off-side

a mix-up between the two batters; but no harm done in the end. This was punched towards cover-point by Philip Salt and the fielder stationed there stopped it with a dive to his right. Salt is sent back by Kohli; and it was the right call as the fielder managed to score a direct-hit at the keeper's end. Salt manages to get back in

opens his account with a rollicking boundary! Back of a length outside off; shaping away form the batter; Philip Salt plays it late; carving it through backward point as the ball races away to the fence in a jiffy
pitched up by the bowler around middle and leg; hint of swing as Philip Salt squeezes it towards the fielder at mid-on

more runs; it's four runs! Slight width on offer outside off; Philip Salt makes it count by smashing this through covers for the second boundary of the over. No need to run for those. Nitish Kumar Reddy has a disappointed look on his face
1 run; drifting down the leg-side; this one clips Philip Salt's pads and rolls towards the left-side of the keeper. They take a leg bye to end the over

ball_event: out Caught by Heinrich Klaasen!! Almost a collision between the two fielders. Jaydev Unadkat strikes in his first over. A length delivery outside off and Philip Salt looks to take him. Takes the aerial route by swinging hard
event_info: but he miscues it towards mid-off. Klaasen calls for it and charges forward to take it. There was another fielder going for the catch who was charging across from cover; but luckily they don't collide; and it's Klaasen who completes the formalities. His shout was pretty loud; but probably wasn't clear due to the background noise. Phil Salt c Heinrich Klaasen b Jaydev Unadkat 8(7) [4s-2]
what a stylish shot to open his account! On the pads and Devdutt Padikkal has flicked this over deep backward square leg for half-a-dozen. A perfect shot to get going
nudged past the bowler towards mid-on for one
Jaydev Unadkat goes short from round the wicket; Virat Kohli rides the bounce and pats it down the pitch

Virat Kohli opens his account with a boundary as well. All the RCB batters have opened their respective accounts with a boundary or six in this run chase. This was a length delivery and Virat Kohli frees his arms. Slices this over wide mid-off and just manages to clear the infield; enough to fetch him four runs
taps this towards cover with soft hands for a single
pitched up by the bowler around middle and leg; flicked through square leg for one

easy pickings! Devdutt Padikkal looks in fine touch already. Quite evident in the couple of shots he has played so far. This was pitched up by the bowler around middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal tickles it away to the fine leg fence for a nonchalant boundary
on a length outside off; Devdutt Padikkal slices it through point for one
1 run; fired on leg; Virat Kohli looks to flick this away; ends up getting an inside edge onto the pads. A single taken

another fine shot. On a length outside off; Devdutt Padikkal plants his front foot forward and thumps this over wide mid-off. He has played at this venue all his life and he's making it count at the start of the season
back of a length outside off; shaping away from the batter; Devdutt Padikkal plays it late; steering it down to third man
good length delivery around middle and leg; nudged towards mid-on by Devdutt Padikkal
full length delivery outside off; Devdutt Padikkal advances down the wicket and slices it over deep backward point; managing to clear the fielder stationed at the edge of the boundary

shot! Back of a length delivery at the stumps; Devdutt Padikkal picks the length pretty early and whips this over mid-wicket. He's finding boundaries at his will for the moment. RCB are off to a superb start; chasing a massive total
tries to cramp the batsman for room; back of a length around middle and leg as Devdutt Padikkal swipes it over backward square leg for one
slower back of a length delivery around off; Virat Kohli pats it with soft hands towards the off-side for a single

just look at the wrist work on that shot. Devdutt Padikkal; you beauty. He advances down the wicket; picks the slower one and flicks it over deep mid-wicket nonchalantly. He does look in sublime touch. Has raced away to 34 off 11 and 50 comes up for RCB as well
good length delivery around middle and leg; Virat Kohli swings across the line; doesn't middle it though

even Virat Kohli joins the party! The Chinnaswamy crowd erupt. On a length around middle and leg; Virat Kohli keeps his eyes on the ball and whips it over deep mid-wicket for a clean six. Runs coming thick and fast

50-run stand up! Virat Kohli advances down the wicket and slashes this slightly short ball through covers for yet another boundary. They are making the most of the powerplay. Chants of Kohli; Kohli all across the Chinnaswamy
pulls this short ball through mid-wicket for one

FOUR; things going from bad to worse. A slower bouncer by Jaydev Unadkat and Devdutt Padikkal fails to get bat on it. Kishan can't gather the ball either as the ball spins away slightly after landing in front of him. Beats the keeper and races away to the fence eventually

1 run; banged in short around middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal looks to swipe across the line; it clips his thigh pad and rolls in front of the keeper. Kohli calls for a run; and they scamper across to complete it
tossed up delivery around middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal goes down on his knee and sweeps this over backward square leg. Greets Harsh Dubey into the attack with a boundary
yorks himself by advancing down the wicket. Squeezes it towards the off-side for one
full and straight on leg; Virat Kohli swings hard and gets an inside edge that deflects off the pads towards the on-side
full and quick on middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal heaves it towards long-on for one
down the wicket Virat Kohli advances and flicks this over wide mid-on; just managing to clear the fielder who was charging back
makes room and drives it towards mid-off. A misfield by the fielder stationed there; results in a single
short and wide outside off; Devdutt Padikkal slaps this over point to keep the runs flowing. No stopping him tonight; he has put SRH on the backfoot with his intent tonight
tad short outside off; sliced in front of point by Devdutt Padikkal
a slower bouncer outside off; good change in pace as Virat Kohli looks to play an upper cut; getting beaten in the process
on a length around middle and leg; worked through the leg-side by Virat Kohli for a couple of runs; a fumble in the deep allowed him to take that second

is that the shot of the evening? For me; it definitely is. All about class. This was pitched up by the bowler; right into the slot; Virat Kohli waits for it to arrive and plays a perfect lofted straight drive. What skills; what class! Has been a fantastic start for RCB - these two batters are making batting look so delightful
advances down the wicket and looks to drag this across the line; gets an inside edge towards the on-side
short ball by Harshal Patel; Devdutt Padikkal drags it through the leg-side for one

appeal for a caught behind; but the umpire isn't interested! This was a yorker length ball outside off and Virat Kohli tried to squeeze it out. Gets beaten as Kishan takes it and they also go up in an appeal; only for the umpire to turn it down

dropped! Klaasen; the culprit on this occasion. Virat Kohli whips it uppishly towards long-on and the fielder charges in from the deep. Guess he misjudged it slightly. Gets his fingers underneath; but the ball doesn't stick; it pops out. How costly will that dropped chance prove to be? Klaasen will hope; not much..

Devdutt Padikkal brings up his fifty! He has been in top-notch form today. This was a length delivery around off and Devdutt Padikkal thumped it right over mid-off. Sublime timing as the ball almost goes the distance. Kohli walks upto him and congratulates him for bringing up the milestone. He would want to continue and convert this fifty into a formidable score though

FOUR; Kishan was superb with the bat; but his keeping has been disappointing tonight. This was a short ball outside off which Devdutt Padikkal failed to connect. Kishan fumbles yet again and gifts away four byes
tad short outside off; punched into the off-side by DDP
100 up for RCB after 8.1 overs. This was fraction short outside off and Virat Kohli slapped it towards long-off for one

fortune favours the brave. Fraction short outside off; Devdutt Padikkal goes for a late-cut and the outside edge beats the fielder stationed at short third man. No chance of stopping that for the fielder

100-run stand up! A tossed up delivery on middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal goes down on his knee and sweeps it over deep backward square leg. Comfortably clears the boundary with this finely connected shot

ball_event: out Caught by Heinrich Klaasen!! End of a rollicking knock by Devdutt Padikkal. What an entertaining innings it was. 61 runs off just 26 balls
event_info: and he walks back to a standing ovation from the crowd and his teammates. This was tad full around off and Devdutt Padikkal went for the aerial route once again. Gets enough elevation; but not distance. Klaasen charges in from long-off and completes a rather easy catch in the outfield. Paddikal has brought the required run rate down to 8-odd with this knock. Devdutt Padikkal c Heinrich Klaasen b Harsh Dubey 61(26) [4s-7 6s-4];
played towards long-on by Patidar as he opens his account
flatter through the air around off; knocked down the pitch by Virat Kohli
pushed to long-on by Rajat Patidar for a single

whippage! Gun shot; by a gun player. Not for the first time has he played such a shot. He has powerful wrists. This was back of a length delivery around middle and leg; Virat Kohli stays in the crease and whips it over deep square leg for a flat six. Makes it look so effortless
dragged through mid-wicket by Virat Kohli for one

captain into the act now. Short of a length outside off; Rajat Patidar rocks back in the crease and carves it through backward point as he stands there and admires his shot. Knew he didn't have to run for that; was so sure about the result
yorker length ball around middle and leg; Rajat Patidar squeezes it towards mid-on
banged in short by Harshal Patel outside off; this was probably a bit too wide
advances down the wicket and drives this through covers for a single by reaching out for it
down the wicket to knock this towards long-off

proving to be one-way traffic at the moment. RCB are racing away towards an emphatic win. Rajat Patidar goes down on his knee and whips this over wide long-on for half-a-dozen. Has been a batting beauty - this wicket
whipped through the leg-side for one
driven towards cover by Virat Kohli as he backs away slightly
downtown! Puts on his dancing shoes; gets to the pitch of the delivery and launches it down the ground; right over the boundary for half-a-dozen
spinning away from the batter outside off; Virat Kohli goes for a drive and misses
you miss; I hit. Eshan Malinga misses the length and Rajat Patidar hits this right over him. Stays put and thumps it with a straight bat face over the bowler's head for a lovely boundary
under pressure Eshan Malinga bowls a short one; tried to keep it slow; but was a wide given
fraction short outside off; Rajat Patidar looks to cut this away; miscues it into the off-side
yet another wide ball by Eshan Malinga. Goes short outside off and once again the ball sails over the batter

what a convincing shot! Another clean strike. Eshan Malinga goes full this time around; right into the slot; Rajat Patidar picks the length to perfection and launches it over long-on for a towering six. Couldn't have hit it any better

slower one attempted; but it's a wide ball outside off. SRH have reviewed the umpire's decision. They think this is inside the tramline. Replays suggest this is clearly outside the guideline. Was close to the bat as Rajat Patidar tried to reach out for it; but UltraEdge confirms there was no bat involved; there was clear daylight

carnage! RCB - the defending champions. They are playing like champions. Living upto all those expectations. Captain leading from the front. A length delivery by Eshan Malinga; right into the slot and it has been smoked over deep mid-wicket by the skipper
50-run stand up! This was worked through the leg-side with soft hands by Rajat Patidar
goes wide outside off and misses the tramline once again. That's the fourth wide of the over
tad short outside off; slapped to mid-off for one. The over finally comes to an end - 22 runs from it
slower ball dug into the pitch; Kohli pulls to deep midwicket

ball_event: out Caught by Harsh Dubey!! Perfect execution of the wide slower one from Payne. Over the wicket
event_info: angles it away and makes Patidar reach for the big shot. His bottom hand comes off and he miscues it straight up off the toe-end. Comfortably taken by Dubey at mid-off. Too little too late for SRH. Rajat Patidar c Harsh Dubey b David Payne 31(12) [4s-2 6s-3];

ball_event: out Caught by Jaydev Unadkat!! Payne has two in two! Short one at the body
event_info: pace off and Jitesh has pulled it straight to deep backward square. Didn't quite get hold of it and was probably high off the bat. Jitesh Sharma c Jaydev Unadkat b David Payne 0(1);
short of length on middle; slower off-cutter; David taps it gently in front of cover and plays out the hattrick ball. Kohli was eager for the single but was sent back
short of length on off; punched to short cover
back of a length on middle and leg; another slower off-cutter and David tucks it through midwicket
slower bumper angled in; holds in the pitch and David has to wait for the pull. Drags it to deep square leg
pace on this time; good length on off; Kohli knocks it down to long-on
full delivery just outside off; David goes hard at it and mistimes it down the ground. It bounces over Unadkat's head and David is through for the single
slower short ball just outside off; Kohli glides it to backward point
short and wide; slower one; Kohli mistimes the cut through point

SRH review a wide call! Slower bumper from round the wicket; loops over David's head and the umpire feels it's too high. Let's see what ball-tracking says. David's height is 1.95 meters and this has gone at 2.04 meters. Onfield call is upheld
slower one; short of length; David drags the pull to deep square leg
slower bouncer angled away; David tries a flat-batted smash but mistimes it to mid-off

Kohli starts the season with a fifty! That's what the crowd has come for; and their hero hasn't disappointed. Slower short ball; he drags the pull to deep midwicket and ambles across while a cheer goes across the ground. Raises his bat to acknowledge the applause

proper tennis-style smash from David! Slower bumper angling away; he goes flat-batted and hits it over extra cover. Fielder misjudges it in the deep. Not sure if it would've been a catch; but surely he could've cut off the boundary had he not taken the longer route
slower bouncer that loops over David's head
carves it over extra cover and it goes all the way! Slower one angled away; David reaches out and gives a glimpse of the enormous power he possesses
pulled away behind square
short of length outside off; Kohli punches through the covers
launched down the ground! Slower one that's right in the slot for Kohli who gets under it with a clean swing through the line
takes on the bumper and gets it over the keeper's head! Pace off into the pitch; gets big on Kohli but he gets away with it. Bit of a top-edge or glove maybe
all class! Gives the charge; makes some room and smashes it over extra cover. Went to the boundary like a bullet

that's it! A target of 202 chased down with more than four overs to spare! As emphatic as it gets from the defending champions who have laid down the marker on the opening night of the season. Kohli puts the finishing touches. Slower bouncer outside off; he reaches out and connects nicely with the upper-cut. No stopping it!
on a length outside off; shapes back in slightly as Travis Head steers it towards point
on a length around leg; Travis Head swings early and the inside edge flicks his pads and rolls towards short fine leg. First run of the match

not out! RCB lose a review in the first over of the match. A back of a length delivery outside off; shapes back away as Abhishek Sharma gets beaten on the outside edge. UltraEdge confirms the same as the bowler starts walking back to his run-up mark

first maximum of IPL 2026! Full and wide outside off; Abhishek Sharma reaches out for it with a slice; gets enough elevation as the ball flies over the fielder at deep backward point. He's off the mark in style
good length delivery outside off; Abhishek Sharma has a swing and a miss. Got beaten due to the movement
that's a juicy full toss outside off; Abhishek Sharma plays a well-timed cover drive; but straight to the fielder stationed at cover-point
good length delivery around middle and leg; Travis Head clips it through the leg-side for a single
advances down the wicket and knocks the length delivery in front of cover-point for a quick run
short of a length outside off; shaping away from the batter as Travis Head runs it down to third man for one
fired down the leg-side by Bhuvneshwar Kumar on the shorter side; called a wide by the umpire
short and quick around leg; Abhishek Sharma gets cramped for room as he looks to swipe this away; gets hit on the thigh pad as the ball lobs towards the keeper
on the shorter side and down the leg-side; another loose delivery by Bhuvneshwar Kumar. Jitesh Sharma had to move across by a long way to gather this; it was so wayward
1 run; cramped up for room again; off the thigh pad towards the leg-side as they take a leg bye

chopped on; almost! Just the kind of luck you need to get going in this tournament. Back of a length delivery around off; was too close to the body; Travis Head looks to slice it away and gets an inside edge that misses the stumps and races away to the fine leg boundary
yet another wide. They have bowled too many wides already. Another wayward ball down the leg-side

ball_event: out Caught by Jitesh Sharma!! Abhishek's poor run continues. He hasn't been at his best since a couple of months. This was a short ball by Jacob Duffy and Abhishek Sharma got hurried up due to the pace. He goes for a pull
event_info: losing his shape in the process. A thick top edge is induced and Jitesh calls for it and circles under the skier to complete a safe catch. A disappointing start for the destructive opener in IPL 2026. Abhishek Sharma c Jitesh Sharma b Jacob Duffy 7(8) [6s-1];
short and wide outside off; steered away through backward point
on a length outside off; punched towards cover by Travis Head
short of a length around off; shapes back in and skids along the deck; Travis Head looks to swipe it across the line; gets beaten on the inside edge

stand and deliver! On a length outside off; tad slower; Travis Head stays put and thumps it over mid-off. Didn't middle it to perfection; otherwise this would have sailed over the boundary. Good strike; nevertheless

ball_event: out Caught by Phil Salt!! Two wickets in the over for Jacob Duffy. What a superb debut for him for RCB already. A short ball by the pacer
event_info: Travis Head goes for a flat pull and Phil Salt at deep backward square leg takes a well-judged reverse cup catch. Both the destructive openers are back to the pavilion within the powerplay. The crowd at Chinnaswamy is absolutely loving this. Travis Head c Phil Salt b Jacob Duffy 11(9) [4s-2];
sublime timing! Back of a length outside off; Ishan Kishan stands tall and punches this through cover-point. Finds the gap as the ball absolutely races away to the fence
tad short outside off; Ishan Kishan rides the bounce and pats it in front of cover-point. Takes a quick run
lovely delivery by Bhuvneshwar Kumar outside off; shaping away from the batter who feels for it and almost nicks it behind
good length delivery around off; skids along the deck as Nitish Kumar Reddy taps it into the off-side
short and wide outside off; Nitish Kumar Reddy cuts it towards point where Duffy puts in a dive to his left and grabs it
back of a length around middle and leg; clips it towards mid-wicket for a single to end the over
tad short around off; some sharp carry as Nitish Kumar Reddy pats it towards point

ball_event: out Caught by Abhinandan Singh!! This is a dream debut for Jacob Duffy. He goes short
event_info: he goes quick and the batter gets hurried. Tries to go across the line; swings with lack of footwork and the top edge has been taken at mid-wicket. Abhinandan takes it stylishly and throws the ball away in swagger. Sunrisers Hyderabad in a spot of bother at the Chinnaswamy. They are three down within the powerplay.. Nitish Reddy c Abhinandan Singh b Jacob Duffy 1(6);
fraction short outside off; shaping away from Heinrich Klaasen who lets it through to the keeper
on a length outside off; Heinrich Klaasen drives this through covers; finds the gap and opens his account. Three runs to get going
fraction short outside off; some extra carry as Ishan Kishan steers this through backward point for one
end of another fantastic over by Jacob Duffy. Drifting on the pads as Heinrich Klaasen clips it with soft hands towards the on-side
short; quick and at the body. Ishan Kishan loses his shape while going for the pull shot; miscues it towards square leg; but it just falls in front of the fielder stationed there

that's the kind of form he has been in. More like it. A short ball on leg; Ishan Kishan picks the length early and pulls it over deep backward square leg for a 89m maximum. SRH need their captain to play with a similar intent tonight
more runs! Banged in short by Abhinandan Singh; Ishan Kishan goes for a pull over mid-wicket and manages to beat Duffy's chase. He put in a dive in the deep; but couldn't quite get there

Abhinandan Singh proving to be very expensive in his first over. This was a short and quick delivery outside off; Ishan Kishan slashes hard at it and the top edge flies right over third man. Hits the ad hoardings beyond the boundary
that was a juicy full toss; Ishan Kishan slices it away; but can't beat the fielder stationed inside the circle on the off-side
16 runs from that over! Back of a length around middle and leg; nudged towards mid-wicket by Ishan Kishan
a short ball by Jacob Duffy and Heinrich Klaasen has pulled it towards deep mid-wicket on the bounce
Jacob Duffy goes short outside off; Ishan Kishan rides the bounce; steering it towards third man for one
fraction short outside off; Heinrich Klaasen steers it through cover-point for one
short of a length around off; Kishan looks to defend; gets an inside edge onto the thigh pad; the ball rolls towards short third

almost another wicket! Heinrich Klaasen closes the bat face early and looks to nudge this away; ends up getting a leading edge for being early in his shot. The leading edge flies straight back to the bowler; and lands just in front of him

end of a superb spell on his RCB debut! Goes short on middle and leg; as Heinrich Klaasen pulls it towards deep square leg on the bounce. The RCB crowd give him a huge cheer at the end of his spell; bowled four overs on the trot. Has done his job with the ball for his new IPL franchise - he has been replaced by Padikkal who is the impact sub
on a length around middle and leg; driven down the ground to long-on
shot! Back of a length outside off; Ishan Kishan gets on top of it and stylishly carves it away through backward point. No chance of stopping that as Kishan adds four more to the tally
good length delivery outside off as Ishan Kishan plays this through covers for a single

yet another boundary. Romario Shepherd goes full around off; Heinrich Klaasen goes deep inside the crease and plays a well-timed off-drive. Beats the fielder inside the circle comfortably and finds the fence with ease
replica of the previous shot! Romario Shepherd goes full again and Heinrich Klaasen executes his off-drive once again. Same shot; same execution; and same result

just the kind of partnership SRH needed to steady the ship. These two batters are exactly doing that. This was fraction short and Heinrich Klaasen swiped it towards wide long-on. Salt in the deep does extremely well to stop the ball which was travelling and saves a boundary
Abhinandan Singh goes short outside off as Ishan Kishan drags it towards the on-side for one
full toss on the pads; Heinrich Klaasen clips it towards wide long-on for one
short in length around off; Ishan Kishan looks to drag this across the line; gets an inside edge onto the pads

smashed! Abhinandan Singh goes short outside off; this was on the slower side; Ishan Kishan picks the length and pace pretty well; rocks back in the crease and pulls it over mid-wicket. There were a couple of fielders converging in the deep; but he just managed to bisect them
worked through the leg-side for a couple of runs
50-run stand up! Pitched up by the bowler outside off; Ishan Kishan looks to squeeze it out; gets an inside edge past the stumps for a single
starts with a googly outside off; Ishan Kishan plays it late; steering it towards backward point

tossed up delivery outside off; slightly widish; Ishan Kishan reaches out for it with a drive and manages to beat Tim David's dive in the deep. The timing and placement were good enough to fetch him four runs
flatter through the air outside off; played through the off-side for a single

fraction short outside off; skids along the deck as Heinrich Klaasen punches it towards extra cover. The fielder stationed there puts in a dive to his right; gets his hand to it; eventually parrying it towards long-off
drags this slightly short ball through backward square leg as the fielder (Kanishk Chouhan) in the deep does well to stop the ball; keeps them down to two
driven back to the bowler to end the over
fired in flat on off; zips through from back of a length; Klaasen is cramped on the cut and mistimes it to short cover. He throws his head back in frustration. Not happy with himself
length ball sliding in on middle; Klaasen punches to long-off

stays round the wicket for the left-hander and angles it away outside off. Oh he bowled it with a very round-arm release while also bending his knees to keep the trajectory low. It's fired wide outside off; Kishan flat-bats to long-off
pushed down to long-off
angled away wide of off; good length ball; Kishan cuts it to deep point
driven firmly down the ground; it's in the air and Krunal dives across for the catch but it's out of his reach. Was much slower through the air at 88kph this delivery
fielder is very early into the dive but still manages to cut it off. Full on the pads; drifting in nicely and Klaasen clips it gently to the left of short fine
drilled down the ground! It's a googly from Suyash but it's too full and Kishan isn't going to let that go unpunished. Brings up his fifty in style!
again the same error. Length is too full and Kishan pounces. Only difference to the previous delivery was that the line was outside off and he went over extra cover instead of going down the ground

mix-up in the running but Klaasen survives! He responded to the initial call of a single from Kishan who later pulled out. Could've been trouble with a better throw but no damage done in the end. Kishan came dancing down the pitch and found the fielder at cover with his drive. Was no single there
can't find the gap as he cuts to backward point
terrible delivery and it disappears to the boundary in no time. Third one of the over. Dragged down short; perhaps anticipated a charge from Kishan but he hangs back and pulls hard behind square
oh that's a gift! Proper gift and nicely wrapped too with a bow on top. Juicy full toss; Klaasen's eyes light up and he smokes it over midwicket
fired in on a good length just outside off; Klaasen cuts through point
flat around off; back of a length; Kishan goes hard on the pull and mistimes it through midwicket
back of a length on middle and off; Klaasen makes some room and cuts to sweeper cover

the round-arm delivery from Krunal and this time it's dispatched over the ropes! Along with being round-arm; was also the 25-yarder from Krunal. Bowled it from well behind the crease and kept it on the pads. But Kishan quickly collapsed his back knee to get under the ball for a big slog-sweep. He's on fire out there!
now goes down the ground and fetches another six! Krunal is wondering where to bowl to him. Goes full outside off; it's in the slot and it gets hammered straight back

ball_event: out Caught by Phil Salt!! Salt is celebrating but he was very close to the ropes there. Umpire will send it upstairs to check if the catch is clean. Back of a length
event_info: heaved across the line and goes very high. It's Salt at the midwicket boundary and he goes down while taking the catch; doesn't stay on his feet. Replays don't seem very conclusive - very hard to say if some part of his back is touching the cushions. I don't see any movement of the cushions; says the third umpire. OUT flashes on the big screen; much to the delight of the partisan home crowd. Klaasen doesn't seem too pleased. He's having a word with the fourth umpire before making his way to the dugout. Heinrich Klaasen c Phil Salt b Romario Shepherd 31(22) [4s-2 6s-1];
tucked gently into the on-side
dug in short; Arora pulls it in the air and it falls just short of deep square leg who was charging in from the boundary
short of length outside off; angling away from over the wicket; Kishan cuts it to sweeper cover

the top-edge flies all the way and Shepherd has a big smile on his face. Can't believe his luck. Dug in short; Arora takes it on again and once again he's not in control of the pull. Gets big on him and flies over fine leg
back of a length in the channel; steered through point
right in the slot but Arora misses out. Drags it off a thick inside edge behind square. Was floated up from Suyash who missed his length again; like he did very often in his previous over
googly across the left-hander; good length ball; Kishan taps it through the off-side

ball_event: out Caught by Devdutt Padikkal !! Not very convincing from Arora in his short stay. Gets dismissed off a half-tracker. Was there to be pulled into the stands but he goes very half-heartedly and finds the fielder at deep midwicket. Good catch from Padikkal moving forward. Salil Arora c Devdutt Padikkal b Suyash Sharma 9(6) [6s-1]

event_info: flat and short outside off; Aniket looks to cut and is beaten
nicely played! Googly outside off; there's width on offer but Aniket doesn't go too hard. Simply opens the face and steers it past short third. Right in the gap between backward point and short third
almost a bouncer from Suyash. Very short outside off; speed is high as well; Aniket looks to pull and hits it down to long-on
good length on off; defended into the off-side

oh he got into such a great position! Abhinandan bashes it on a short of length and Aniket pounces! His weight is on the back foot as he pulls it for a flat six over midwicket. Was barely a short-arm jab but the timing was awesome
cracking yorker around off; squeezed to backward point
this time he gets rushed by the short ball. It zips through; hurries onto him and he gets it high on the bat while going for the pull. It's miscued in the air but lands safely wide of deep midwicket
such special batting! Just another level from Kishan! Angling away outside off; back of a length; he opens the face and carves it over short third. Flies over the fielder and then over the ropes

ball_event: out Caught by Phil Salt!! Phil Salt
event_info: you beauty! It needed something special to get rid of Kishan tonight and he has produced just that. A one-handed screamer running across at the point boundary when the ball seemed destined for the ropes. Full toss outside off; Kishan reaches out; opens the face and slices it in the air. It's well wide of Salt who charges to his right before reaching out one-handed at full stretch. Incredible work. Ishan Kishan c Phil Salt b Abhinandan Singh 80(38) [4s-8 6s-5];

makes this look so effortless! Back of a length; pace off perhaps and it sits up around the waist on middle and leg. From there it's all about the elegance of Aniket. Swivels across beautifully and pulls it over fine leg without going too hard. Pure timing!
tucked away into the on-side
close to the blockhole just outside off; sliced through point
dug in short just outside off; pace off; Aniket pulls it to deep square leg
slower bumper angled away outside off; left alone by Dubey and the umpire reckons it's outside the marker
short of length outside off; pace off again and Dubey drags the pull shot through midwicket. It's in the gap and he's back for two

ball_event: out Caught by Devdutt Padikkal!! Looks like Shepherd has outsmarted Dubey here. He shuffled across the stumps premeditatedly
event_info: probably expecting the line to be wide. But what he got was a very full ball on leg stump. Flicks it in the air and finds the fielder at fine leg. Harsh Dubey c Devdutt Padikkal b Romario Shepherd 3(3);
pace off looks like the way to go in these last few overs for RCB. Slower one outside off; back of a length and Aniket cuts it to sweeper cover

the slower bouncer from Bhuvi and it's been ramped over the keeper. Aniket initially took a couple of steps down the pitch; which may have prompted Bhuvi to go short. But he still managed to adjust so well

dropped by Kohli! Ran in from long-on; got to it but the ball burst right through his hands and landed on the thigh. He looks surprised more than anything. Was full in the slot; Aniket tried a no-look shot but miscued it high up. Lucky to get away!
short of length outside off ; pace off; Harshal punches it to cover

ball_event: out Caught by Devdutt Padikkal!! Straight down the throat of Padikkal at long-off. No mistake from him and Harshal has to go. Short of length outside off
event_info: he tries lofting off the back foot; not an easy shot and there's not enough on it to clear the ropes. The wickets keep tumbling. Harshal Patel c Devdutt Padikkal b Bhuvneshwar Kumar 0(2);
pace on this time; good length at the stumps; Payne knocks it down to long-off
finds the blockhole; Payne can't do much except squeeze it down to long-on
massive hit down the ground! In the slot around off; he gets under it and whacks it over long-on. Nice stable base to go with a hefty swing
tries the slower short ball this time but it's all the same for Aniket who spots it early; waits for it and plays the upper-cut to perfection. Almost goes all the way over backward point
not great bowling. Again in the slot; misses the yorker by a long way and Aniket takes full toll. Giving SRH just the finish they want. Smashes it down the ground for six more

review taken by RCB for a wide call! It's full and wide; Aniket tries a reverse-scoop but misses out. He had walked across the stumps and was standing well outside off when the ball was released. The revised wide marker has moved accordingly; and it's well inside the line according to ball-tracking. Good review!

ball_event: out Caught by Virat Kohli!! Was he caught in two minds? Looked like a very half-hearted chip down the ground. Was probably thinking if he should take a single to keep strike or go for the big hit. Ended up chipping it to long-off. Was full in the slot
event_info: there to be punished; but it results in his dismissal. Simple catch for Kohli at long-off. Aniket has a smile on his face as he walks off. Aniket Verma c Virat Kohli b Romario Shepherd 43(18) [4s-3 6s-4];
keeps it wide outside off; Payne goes after it and gets it away in the gap through point
again wide outside off; Payne slices it wide of sweeper cover; back for two more
1 run; now the yorker angled back in; catches Payne by surprise; his head falls over and he's hit on the pad. Was missing leg!
full and straight; driven firmly down the ground and long-off does well to cut it off
133kph; nails the yorker at the stumps; Unadkat squeezes it back down the pitch
close to the blockhole on off; Unadkat can't get under it but he drives wide of long-off and is back for two. Very poor throw from the fielder there. Should've been a run-out but the throw is so wide
short of a length around off; Philip Salt defends it into the off-side

a mix-up between the two batters; but no harm done in the end. This was punched towards cover-point by Philip Salt and the fielder stationed there stopped it with a dive to his right. Salt is sent back by Kohli; and it was the right call as the fielder managed to score a direct-hit at the keeper's end. Salt manages to get back in

opens his account with a rollicking boundary! Back of a length outside off; shaping away form the batter; Philip Salt plays it late; carving it through backward point as the ball races away to the fence in a jiffy
pitched up by the bowler around middle and leg; hint of swing as Philip Salt squeezes it towards the fielder at mid-on

more runs; it's four runs! Slight width on offer outside off; Philip Salt makes it count by smashing this through covers for the second boundary of the over. No need to run for those. Nitish Kumar Reddy has a disappointed look on his face
1 run; drifting down the leg-side; this one clips Philip Salt's pads and rolls towards the left-side of the keeper. They take a leg bye to end the over

ball_event: out Caught by Heinrich Klaasen!! Almost a collision between the two fielders. Jaydev Unadkat strikes in his first over. A length delivery outside off and Philip Salt looks to take him. Takes the aerial route by swinging hard
event_info: but he miscues it towards mid-off. Klaasen calls for it and charges forward to take it. There was another fielder going for the catch who was charging across from cover; but luckily they don't collide; and it's Klaasen who completes the formalities. His shout was pretty loud; but probably wasn't clear due to the background noise. Phil Salt c Heinrich Klaasen b Jaydev Unadkat 8(7) [4s-2]
what a stylish shot to open his account! On the pads and Devdutt Padikkal has flicked this over deep backward square leg for half-a-dozen. A perfect shot to get going
nudged past the bowler towards mid-on for one
Jaydev Unadkat goes short from round the wicket; Virat Kohli rides the bounce and pats it down the pitch

Virat Kohli opens his account with a boundary as well. All the RCB batters have opened their respective accounts with a boundary or six in this run chase. This was a length delivery and Virat Kohli frees his arms. Slices this over wide mid-off and just manages to clear the infield; enough to fetch him four runs
taps this towards cover with soft hands for a single
pitched up by the bowler around middle and leg; flicked through square leg for one

easy pickings! Devdutt Padikkal looks in fine touch already. Quite evident in the couple of shots he has played so far. This was pitched up by the bowler around middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal tickles it away to the fine leg fence for a nonchalant boundary
on a length outside off; Devdutt Padikkal slices it through point for one
1 run; fired on leg; Virat Kohli looks to flick this away; ends up getting an inside edge onto the pads. A single taken

another fine shot. On a length outside off; Devdutt Padikkal plants his front foot forward and thumps this over wide mid-off. He has played at this venue all his life and he's making it count at the start of the season
back of a length outside off; shaping away from the batter; Devdutt Padikkal plays it late; steering it down to third man
good length delivery around middle and leg; nudged towards mid-on by Devdutt Padikkal
full length delivery outside off; Devdutt Padikkal advances down the wicket and slices it over deep backward point; managing to clear the fielder stationed at the edge of the boundary

shot! Back of a length delivery at the stumps; Devdutt Padikkal picks the length pretty early and whips this over mid-wicket. He's finding boundaries at his will for the moment. RCB are off to a superb start; chasing a massive total
tries to cramp the batsman for room; back of a length around middle and leg as Devdutt Padikkal swipes it over backward square leg for one
slower back of a length delivery around off; Virat Kohli pats it with soft hands towards the off-side for a single

just look at the wrist work on that shot. Devdutt Padikkal; you beauty. He advances down the wicket; picks the slower one and flicks it over deep mid-wicket nonchalantly. He does look in sublime touch. Has raced away to 34 off 11 and 50 comes up for RCB as well
good length delivery around middle and leg; Virat Kohli swings across the line; doesn't middle it though

even Virat Kohli joins the party! The Chinnaswamy crowd erupt. On a length around middle and leg; Virat Kohli keeps his eyes on the ball and whips it over deep mid-wicket for a clean six. Runs coming thick and fast

50-run stand up! Virat Kohli advances down the wicket and slashes this slightly short ball through covers for yet another boundary. They are making the most of the powerplay. Chants of Kohli; Kohli all across the Chinnaswamy
pulls this short ball through mid-wicket for one

FOUR; things going from bad to worse. A slower bouncer by Jaydev Unadkat and Devdutt Padikkal fails to get bat on it. Kishan can't gather the ball either as the ball spins away slightly after landing in front of him. Beats the keeper and races away to the fence eventually

1 run; banged in short around middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal looks to swipe across the line; it clips his thigh pad and rolls in front of the keeper. Kohli calls for a run; and they scamper across to complete it
tossed up delivery around middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal goes down on his knee and sweeps this over backward square leg. Greets Harsh Dubey into the attack with a boundary
yorks himself by advancing down the wicket. Squeezes it towards the off-side for one
full and straight on leg; Virat Kohli swings hard and gets an inside edge that deflects off the pads towards the on-side
full and quick on middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal heaves it towards long-on for one
down the wicket Virat Kohli advances and flicks this over wide mid-on; just managing to clear the fielder who was charging back
makes room and drives it towards mid-off. A misfield by the fielder stationed there; results in a single
short and wide outside off; Devdutt Padikkal slaps this over point to keep the runs flowing. No stopping him tonight; he has put SRH on the backfoot with his intent tonight
tad short outside off; sliced in front of point by Devdutt Padikkal
a slower bouncer outside off; good change in pace as Virat Kohli looks to play an upper cut; getting beaten in the process
on a length around middle and leg; worked through the leg-side by Virat Kohli for a couple of runs; a fumble in the deep allowed him to take that second

is that the shot of the evening? For me; it definitely is. All about class. This was pitched up by the bowler; right into the slot; Virat Kohli waits for it to arrive and plays a perfect lofted straight drive. What skills; what class! Has been a fantastic start for RCB - these two batters are making batting look so delightful
advances down the wicket and looks to drag this across the line; gets an inside edge towards the on-side
short ball by Harshal Patel; Devdutt Padikkal drags it through the leg-side for one

appeal for a caught behind; but the umpire isn't interested! This was a yorker length ball outside off and Virat Kohli tried to squeeze it out. Gets beaten as Kishan takes it and they also go up in an appeal; only for the umpire to turn it down

dropped! Klaasen; the culprit on this occasion. Virat Kohli whips it uppishly towards long-on and the fielder charges in from the deep. Guess he misjudged it slightly. Gets his fingers underneath; but the ball doesn't stick; it pops out. How costly will that dropped chance prove to be? Klaasen will hope; not much..

Devdutt Padikkal brings up his fifty! He has been in top-notch form today. This was a length delivery around off and Devdutt Padikkal thumped it right over mid-off. Sublime timing as the ball almost goes the distance. Kohli walks upto him and congratulates him for bringing up the milestone. He would want to continue and convert this fifty into a formidable score though

FOUR; Kishan was superb with the bat; but his keeping has been disappointing tonight. This was a short ball outside off which Devdutt Padikkal failed to connect. Kishan fumbles yet again and gifts away four byes
tad short outside off; punched into the off-side by DDP
100 up for RCB after 8.1 overs. This was fraction short outside off and Virat Kohli slapped it towards long-off for one

fortune favours the brave. Fraction short outside off; Devdutt Padikkal goes for a late-cut and the outside edge beats the fielder stationed at short third man. No chance of stopping that for the fielder

100-run stand up! A tossed up delivery on middle and leg; Devdutt Padikkal goes down on his knee and sweeps it over deep backward square leg. Comfortably clears the boundary with this finely connected shot

ball_event: out Caught by Heinrich Klaasen!! End of a rollicking knock by Devdutt Padikkal. What an entertaining innings it was. 61 runs off just 26 balls
event_info: and he walks back to a standing ovation from the crowd and his teammates. This was tad full around off and Devdutt Padikkal went for the aerial route once again. Gets enough elevation; but not distance. Klaasen charges in from long-off and completes a rather easy catch in the outfield. Paddikal has brought the required run rate down to 8-odd with this knock. Devdutt Padikkal c Heinrich Klaasen b Harsh Dubey 61(26) [4s-7 6s-4];
played towards long-on by Patidar as he opens his account
flatter through the air around off; knocked down the pitch by Virat Kohli
pushed to long-on by Rajat Patidar for a single

whippage! Gun shot; by a gun player. Not for the first time has he played such a shot. He has powerful wrists. This was back of a length delivery around middle and leg; Virat Kohli stays in the crease and whips it over deep square leg for a flat six. Makes it look so effortless
dragged through mid-wicket by Virat Kohli for one

captain into the act now. Short of a length outside off; Rajat Patidar rocks back in the crease and carves it through backward point as he stands there and admires his shot. Knew he didn't have to run for that; was so sure about the result
yorker length ball around middle and leg; Rajat Patidar squeezes it towards mid-on
banged in short by Harshal Patel outside off; this was probably a bit too wide
advances down the wicket and drives this through covers for a single by reaching out for it
down the wicket to knock this towards long-off

proving to be one-way traffic at the moment. RCB are racing away towards an emphatic win. Rajat Patidar goes down on his knee and whips this over wide long-on for half-a-dozen. Has been a batting beauty - this wicket
whipped through the leg-side for one
driven towards cover by Virat Kohli as he backs away slightly
downtown! Puts on his dancing shoes; gets to the pitch of the delivery and launches it down the ground; right over the boundary for half-a-dozen
spinning away from the batter outside off; Virat Kohli goes for a drive and misses
you miss; I hit. Eshan Malinga misses the length and Rajat Patidar hits this right over him. Stays put and thumps it with a straight bat face over the bowler's head for a lovely boundary
under pressure Eshan Malinga bowls a short one; tried to keep it slow; but was a wide given
fraction short outside off; Rajat Patidar looks to cut this away; miscues it into the off-side
yet another wide ball by Eshan Malinga. Goes short outside off and once again the ball sails over the batter

what a convincing shot! Another clean strike. Eshan Malinga goes full this time around; right into the slot; Rajat Patidar picks the length to perfection and launches it over long-on for a towering six. Couldn't have hit it any better

slower one attempted; but it's a wide ball outside off. SRH have reviewed the umpire's decision. They think this is inside the tramline. Replays suggest this is clearly outside the guideline. Was close to the bat as Rajat Patidar tried to reach out for it; but UltraEdge confirms there was no bat involved; there was clear daylight

carnage! RCB - the defending champions. They are playing like champions. Living upto all those expectations. Captain leading from the front. A length delivery by Eshan Malinga; right into the slot and it has been smoked over deep mid-wicket by the skipper
50-run stand up! This was worked through the leg-side with soft hands by Rajat Patidar
goes wide outside off and misses the tramline once again. That's the fourth wide of the over
tad short outside off; slapped to mid-off for one. The over finally comes to an end - 22 runs from it
slower ball dug into the pitch; Kohli pulls to deep midwicket

ball_event: out Caught by Harsh Dubey!! Perfect execution of the wide slower one from Payne. Over the wicket
event_info: angles it away and makes Patidar reach for the big shot. His bottom hand comes off and he miscues it straight up off the toe-end. Comfortably taken by Dubey at mid-off. Too little too late for SRH. Rajat Patidar c Harsh Dubey b David Payne 31(12) [4s-2 6s-3];

ball_event: out Caught by Jaydev Unadkat!! Payne has two in two! Short one at the body
event_info: pace off and Jitesh has pulled it straight to deep backward square. Didn't quite get hold of it and was probably high off the bat. Jitesh Sharma c Jaydev Unadkat b David Payne 0(1);
short of length on middle; slower off-cutter; David taps it gently in front of cover and plays out the hattrick ball. Kohli was eager for the single but was sent back
short of length on off; punched to short cover
back of a length on middle and leg; another slower off-cutter and David tucks it through midwicket
slower bumper angled in; holds in the pitch and David has to wait for the pull. Drags it to deep square leg
pace on this time; good length on off; Kohli knocks it down to long-on
full delivery just outside off; David goes hard at it and mistimes it down the ground. It bounces over Unadkat's head and David is through for the single
slower short ball just outside off; Kohli glides it to backward point
short and wide; slower one; Kohli mistimes the cut through point

SRH review a wide call! Slower bumper from round the wicket; loops over David's head and the umpire feels it's too high. Let's see what ball-tracking says. David's height is 1.95 meters and this has gone at 2.04 meters. Onfield call is upheld
slower one; short of length; David drags the pull to deep square leg
slower bouncer angled away; David tries a flat-batted smash but mistimes it to mid-off

Kohli starts the season with a fifty! That's what the crowd has come for; and their hero hasn't disappointed. Slower short ball; he drags the pull to deep midwicket and ambles across while a cheer goes across the ground. Raises his bat to acknowledge the applause

proper tennis-style smash from David! Slower bumper angling away; he goes flat-batted and hits it over extra cover. Fielder misjudges it in the deep. Not sure if it would've been a catch; but surely he could've cut off the boundary had he not taken the longer route
slower bouncer that loops over David's head
carves it over extra cover and it goes all the way! Slower one angled away; David reaches out and gives a glimpse of the enormous power he possesses
pulled away behind square
short of length outside off; Kohli punches through the covers
launched down the ground! Slower one that's right in the slot for Kohli who gets under it with a clean swing through the line
takes on the bumper and gets it over the keeper's head! Pace off into the pitch; gets big on Kohli but he gets away with it. Bit of a top-edge or glove maybe
all class! Gives the charge; makes some room and smashes it over extra cover. Went to the boundary like a bullet

that's it! A target of 202 chased down with more than four overs to spare! As emphatic as it gets from the defending champions who have laid down the marker on the opening night of the season. Kohli puts the finishing touches. Slower bouncer outside off; he reaches out and connects nicely with the upper-cut. No stopping it!
